jokes.text: Well, we got one, but ours is in the men's room.
jokes.setup: Immigration officers asking for Louie De Palma
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Louie being in the bathroom, treating it like a mundane location detail
jokes.explanation: Good economy and structure - sets up expectation of formal response, delivers casual mundane detail instead

jokes.text: How far did you get? / 'Dear Allen.' / That's 'Alien.'
jokes.setup: Someone asking how far Latka got translating his immigration letter
jokes.punchline: He only got through the salutation and misread 'Alien' as 'Allen'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor that reveals both Latka's language barrier and naive optimism in a perfectly structured way

jokes.text: Papers? / Zigzags?
jokes.setup: Alex asking if Latka has his immigration papers
jokes.punchline: Latka thinks he means rolling papers for cigarettes
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word delivers the entire joke. Shows Latka's cultural disconnect in an unexpected way

jokes.text: You ever see any of the letters Latka gets from his homeland? They have postage stamps that honor barbed wire.
jokes.setup: Suggesting deportation isn't so bad since Latka would go home
jokes.punchline: Revealing his homeland is apparently a dystopian nightmare through stamp imagery
jokes.explanation: Clever dark humor that builds world through specific absurd detail. 'Honoring barbed wire' is a perfectly crafted image

jokes.text: Some of it ain't even true.
jokes.setup: Louie complaining about disgusting graffiti written about him
jokes.punchline: Implying some of the disgusting things ARE true
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie character moment - accidentally admitting to his own flaws while complaining

jokes.text: You should see what's written about you on the walls of the ladies' room. / I know what it says in there. I wrote it.
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting Louie see what's written about him in the women's restroom
jokes.punchline: Louie admitting he wrote it himself
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and character reveal - Louie's narcissism extends to writing his own graffiti

jokes.text: Latka's the poodle I never had.
jokes.setup: Louie being accused of loving Latka and deciding to help him
jokes.punchline: Comparing Latka to a pet poodle
jokes.explanation: Perfect character line - Louie's twisted way of expressing affection while maintaining his gruff exterior

jokes.text: I don't think this is a full deck. / Doesn't make any difference. They'll never know.
jokes.setup: Someone noting the cards they're using for their fake game aren't complete
jokes.punchline: Assuming the federal agents won't notice the obvious deception
jokes.explanation: Decent situational humor about their incompetent cover-up attempt

jokes.text: They like to be called officers.
jokes.setup: Someone about to address the immigration agents
jokes.punchline: Correcting the terminology while they're in the middle of hiding a fugitive
jokes.explanation: Nice character touch - Alex's politeness even in crisis situations

jokes.text: He's under that cab. / Louie! / You swine!
jokes.setup: Everyone working to hide Latka from immigration
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately betraying him without hesitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - his self-preservation trumps any loyalty, delivered with zero hesitation

jokes.text: Fifth Avenue and... I don't know that intersection.
jokes.setup: Passenger giving taxi directions
jokes.punchline: Not knowing where they want to go after stating a specific street
jokes.explanation: Minor observational humor about typical taxi passenger confusion

jokes.text: You take tongue right out of my mouth.
jokes.setup: Alex explaining how marriage would solve Latka's immigration problem
jokes.punchline: Latka's mangled version of 'you took the words right out of my mouth'
jokes.explanation: Great Latka malapropism that's both endearing and creates a vivid wrong image

jokes.text: Phyllis George.
jokes.setup: Asked if he has ideas for women to marry
jokes.punchline: Naming a famous beauty queen/TV personality as his realistic option
jokes.explanation: Shows Latka's naive optimism and disconnect from reality in character-appropriate way

jokes.text: That's why I was buying you time when the feds came in.
jokes.setup: Louie claiming he helped Latka escape
jokes.punchline: Rewriting history since he immediately betrayed Latka
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie self-deception, rewriting his betrayal as heroic assistance

jokes.text: It never occurred to him to just drop it.
jokes.setup: Louie continuing to try bribing Latka despite being ignored
jokes.punchline: Noting Louie's inability to recognize when his approach isn't working
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Louie's tunnel vision and inability to read social cues

jokes.text: That's the sweetest thing anybody ever said to me. I think.
jokes.setup: Latka presumably proposing to Elaine in his native language
jokes.punchline: Elaine's uncertainty about whether what she couldn't understand was actually sweet
jokes.explanation: Nice structure with the delayed doubt undercutting the romantic moment

jokes.text: Who asked you?
jokes.setup: Someone assuring her she wouldn't have to sleep with Latka
jokes.punchline: Elaine's indignant response to the unsolicited reassurance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Elaine's pride being wounded by the assumption

jokes.text: Oh, boy, we will be so happy. / No. N-o spells 'no.' / Aw, shup.
jokes.setup: Elaine clearly rejecting Latka's proposal multiple times
jokes.punchline: Latka hearing what he wants to hear and dismissing her final rejection
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Latka's selective hearing and endearing persistence

jokes.text: She's not a hooker. She's a call girl.
jokes.setup: Someone objecting to Latka marrying 'this hooker'
jokes.punchline: Alex making a technical distinction without addressing the core concern
jokes.explanation: Shows Alex's tendency to focus on technicalities when uncomfortable with bigger picture

jokes.text: I love you.
jokes.setup: Latka meeting his arranged bride Vivian for the first time
jokes.punchline: Immediately declaring love to a woman he just met who's doing this as a business transaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Latka's romantic nature clashing with reality

jokes.text: I don't understand why he won't forgive me. All I did was turn him in.
jokes.setup: Louie wondering why Latka is angry with him
jokes.punchline: Completely minimizing his betrayal as if it were a minor thing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - genuinely confused about consequences of his selfishness

jokes.text: But it's not absolutely necessary.
jokes.setup: John saying communication is the most important thing in marriage
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining his own marriage advice
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific humor revealing John's own marital issues through advice

jokes.text: The line to kiss the bride could take a while. / I hear they asked the vice squad to give the bride away.
jokes.setup: Joking about the unusual wedding
jokes.punchline: Escalating with increasingly inappropriate jokes about the bride's profession
jokes.explanation: Decent setup-punchline structure but relies on easy targets rather than clever insight

jokes.text: I've dressed up as everything from Pocahontas to Darth Vader.
jokes.setup: Vivian agreeing to wear a wedding dress
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing the range of her professional costume work
jokes.explanation: Good specificity in the range from historical figure to sci-fi villain shows breadth of her work

jokes.text: 'How...' / Can anybody else answer for him? / 'How...' / I'm up for retirement in two years.
jokes.setup: Latka struggling to answer immigration officer's question
jokes.punchline: The mounting frustration as Latka can only say 'How' while the officer's career deadline adds pressure
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of bureaucratic frustration with personal stakes added

jokes.text: She's classy, and you're just a grease monkey. It'll never work out.
jokes.setup: Louie fabricating a love story to convince immigration
jokes.punchline: Describing a call girl as 'classy' while insulting Latka
jokes.explanation: Great irony in Louie's upside-down value system and his casual cruelty to Latka

jokes.text: In fact, they even went so far as to offer Latka $1,000 to leave their daughter alone, but he wouldn't take it.
jokes.setup: Louie building his fabricated love story
jokes.punchline: Creating an elaborate backstory involving family opposition and bribery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the lie with specific details that make it seem more believable

jokes.text: The parents only offered me a lousy 50 bucks. / What was the other thing? / I took it.
jokes.setup: Louie revealing his love story was based on personal experience with two changes
jokes.punchline: The other change being that he actually took the bribe to leave the girl
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with perfect setup and devastating character revelation. Shows Louie's complete moral bankruptcy with great economy

jokes.text: The church was investigated and cleared completely. / Cleared of what? / Why go into it?
jokes.setup: Jim assuring them his ordination is legitimate by mentioning investigation
jokes.punchline: Refusing to explain what the church was investigated for
jokes.explanation: Good structure where the reassurance actually creates more doubt. Classic Jim character moment

jokes.text: It doesn't even exist anymore.
jokes.setup: Jim defending the legitimacy of his ordaining church
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning the church no longer exists
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that further undermines his credibility while maintaining his oblivious confidence

jokes.text: Always. This is my first.
jokes.setup: Asked how long it's been since he performed a wedding
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's never done one before by saying 'always'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim logic - answering literally but creating confusion. Great economy and character consistency

jokes.text: My mother paid good money for these lessons, and I thought Latka should have some music for his nuptials.
jokes.setup: Louie presumably playing some kind of instrument badly
jokes.punchline: Justifying his performance by invoking his mother's investment and Latka's needs
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Louie's unexpected sentimentality and pride in his limited talents

jokes.text: I bet you all thought I was going to screw up, didn't you?
jokes.setup: Jim delivering an unexpectedly beautiful wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall to acknowledge the character expectations
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment that acknowledges both character development and audience expectations

jokes.text: 'A part of me.'
jokes.setup: Reading Latka's vows which say 'you will always be a tart to me'
jokes.punchline: Correcting the mispronunciation to the intended romantic meaning
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Latka's language struggles creating unintended meanings in important moments

jokes.text: This is heavy.
jokes.setup: Completing a touching wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Jim's casual, understated reaction to the emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim response - casual drug-era language for a profound emotional moment

jokes.text: No honeymoon?
jokes.setup: Vivian leaving immediately after the wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Latka's innocent question revealing he still doesn't understand this is purely business
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Latka's romantic innocence clashing with harsh reality. Great economy

jokes.text: Boy, America's a tough town.
jokes.setup: Latka's wedding day ending with his bride immediately leaving
jokes.punchline: His understated conclusion about American culture
jokes.explanation: Perfect character conclusion - Latka's innocent perspective on his harsh welcome to America

jokes.text: What did you do today? / Oh, uh... meet girl. Marry her. Uh, she leave me. It was fun.
jokes.setup: Someone asking Latka about his day
jokes.punchline: Casually summarizing the entire dramatic episode as 'fun'
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode-ending button. Latka's resilient optimism condensed into perfect economy. Sums up entire episode in character-perfect way
